description: Repeating a failing command without new evidence wastes debugging budget.
body:
Rerun only when input, environment, instrumentation, or hypothesis changed.

Use only as a diagnostic do-not-use signal; do not expose this as positive strategy, methodology, or memory. Use when a proposed approach skips reproduction steps, stack traces, logs, state transitions, and controlled hypotheses or claims success without domain evidence. Do not use this dossier as a positive implementation strategy; it is a do-not-use signal. Actions: block the shortcut, name the missing evidence, and switch to a safer methodology or ask for human approval. Validation signals include absence or contradiction in minimal repro, before/after trace, regression test, and command output. Risk boundary: treat repeated recurrence as a blocker when it can cause blind retries, wrapper fixes, broad rewrites, and unproven root cause.
